title: Spiesia schrenkii
taxonomy: Spiesia schrenkii is classified in the family Fabaceae. It belongs to the tribe Cercideae and is closely related to other species in the Cercis genus. The genus Spiesia is sometimes considered a synonym for the genus Cercis. However, some sources still recognize Spiesia as a distinct genus. intro: Spiesia schrenkii, also known as Schrenk's caraway, is a perennial plant belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae). The plant is valued for its seeds, which have been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Schrenk's caraway seeds include carvone, limonene, and other sesquiterpenes, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Schrenk's caraway is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or oil, and is also used as a spice in culinary applications.
